title: new tedious Connection with options.trustServerCertificate: true — the SQL Server TLS certificate is not validated (MITM)

params.passDetail: The tedious Connection validates the server certificate (options.trustServerCertificate is not true) — the SQL Server connection's TLS identity is verified.
params.failDetail: new tedious Connection is created with options: { trustServerCertificate: true }, which makes the driver trust ANY certificate the SQL Server presents. A network attacker can present a forged certificate and man-in-the-middle the connection — exposing every query, credential, and record. Remove trustServerCertificate: true; install and trust the server's real CA instead.
params.absentCallDetail: No tedious Connection construction in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: The tedious Connection does not set options.trustServerCertificate; the secure default (validate) applies.

provenance.note: tedious is the driver under mssql. trustServerCertificate: true is pasted from a 'fix the self-signed cert error' answer and ships, disabling MITM protection on the SQL Server connection.
